question_text: Which of the following is a common social benefit most typically associated with regular participation in group exercise classes or team sports for adults?

options.text: Opportunities to develop new friendships and social support networks
Complete social isolation from other human beings forever
A guaranteed increase in personal conflict with strangers
Permanent elimination of family relationships of the exerciser

explanation: Regular group exercise and team sport participation offer opportunities to develop new friendships and social support networks; these social connections in turn reinforce exercise adherence, improve mental wellbeing and contribute to overall life satisfaction for adult participants.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Isolation is the opposite of a social benefit.
Healthy group sport does not guarantee personal conflict.
Exercise does not eliminate family relationships.

question_text: Which of the following is the most appropriate single word that best summarises the core element that distinguishes aggression from unintended contact in sport?

options.text: Intention to harm another person
Colour of the playing uniform
Height of the athletes involved
Location of the stadium on the map

explanation: Intention to harm is the core defining element that distinguishes aggression from unintended contact in sport; when contact occurs accidentally or as part of legitimate play without intent to injure, it is not classified as aggression in sport psychology.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Uniform colour has nothing to do with aggression.
Player height is unrelated to the definition of aggression.
Stadium location does not determine aggression.

question_text: Which of the following best represents the main reason that many adults fail to maintain consistent exercise adherence across a full year as reported in health psychology surveys?

options.text: Time constraints, lack of motivation and low perceived enjoyment
Belief that exercise provides no physical or mental health benefits
Complete inability to move any muscle of the body
Permanent ban on exercise imposed by local government

explanation: Common reasons cited by adults for dropping out of exercise programmes include time constraints in daily life, low motivation, low perceived enjoyment, fatigue and lack of visible progress; these barriers can be addressed with practical strategies tailored to each participant.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Research clearly supports the benefits of exercise in adults.
Inability to move any muscle is a rare, severe medical condition.
A blanket government ban on exercise is not realistic.

question_text: Which of the following strategies is most appropriate for improving exercise adherence among school children within a structured physical education programme at school?

options.text: Introduce variety, fun games and age-appropriate challenges
Force every child to perform only identical maximum-effort drills
Publicly shame children who cannot meet the set standards
Remove all warm-up and cool-down from every single session

explanation: Introducing variety, fun games and age-appropriate challenges supports children's enjoyment, autonomy and perceived competence; these experiences build long-term interest in physical activity and directly address several well-known barriers to adherence in younger populations over time.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Forcing identical maximum-effort drills damages enjoyment.
Public shaming harms motivation and mental health.
Removing warm-up and cool-down raises injury risks.

question_text: Which of the following environmental factors is commonly associated with increased risk of aggressive behaviour during a competitive match according to sport psychology research?

options.text: Crowd hostility and very high-stakes rivalry between teams
A calm empty stadium without any audience at all
Low-stakes friendly match between close teammates
Strictly enforced fair play officiating throughout

explanation: Environmental factors linked to higher aggression include crowd hostility, high-stakes rivalry, provocative chanting and perceived unfair officiating; these factors heighten arousal and reduce behavioural control, making aggressive acts more likely during closely contested matches.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: A calm empty stadium typically reduces arousal and aggression.
Friendly low-stakes matches generally lower aggression levels.
Strict fair-play officiating reduces aggressive behaviour.

question_text: Which of the following is the best way for a coach to respond to a player who has shown a hostile aggressive act during a youth football match on the field?

options.text: Publicly praise the act to encourage more toughness in the team
Immediately remove the player and address behaviour with education
Ignore the incident and continue the match normally
Blame the opposing team even though the act was clearly one-sided

explanation: Best practice is to immediately remove the player from the match, allow time for emotional regulation, address the behaviour with education about fair play and consequences, and agree on steps to prevent future occurrences; ignoring or praising hostile acts teaches wrong values.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Publicly praising hostile acts teaches harmful values.
Ignoring aggression allows it to recur and escalate.
Blaming the other team avoids individual responsibility.

question_text: Which of the following is the most accurate general statement about the relationship between regular moderate exercise and mental health in the general population?

options.text: Regular moderate exercise is associated with improvements in mood and reduced anxiety
Regular moderate exercise always worsens mental health significantly
There is no relationship between exercise and mental health of any kind
Mental health is only related to diet and genetics, never to exercise

explanation: A consistent body of research shows that regular moderate exercise is associated with improvements in mood, reduced symptoms of mild-to-moderate anxiety and depression, better sleep and enhanced self-esteem; these effects complement other treatments and support overall mental wellbeing.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Exercise does not universally worsen mental health.
There is clearly a documented link between exercise and mood.
Mental health depends on many factors, including exercise.

question_text: Which of the following best describes the frustration-aggression hypothesis as commonly summarised in sport psychology literature on athlete behaviour?

options.text: Frustration arising from blocked goals increases the likelihood of aggressive behaviour
Frustration and aggression are always completely unrelated to each other
Only biological factors determine aggression without psychological input
Only aggressive people can feel frustrated in sporting contexts

explanation: The classical frustration-aggression hypothesis suggests that frustration arising from blocked goals increases the likelihood of aggressive behaviour; modern refinements note that frustration does not always lead to aggression, but the link remains a key influence on athlete behaviour under pressure.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: The hypothesis explicitly links frustration with aggression.
Biological factors are important but not the whole story.
Most people feel frustration, not only aggressive ones.

question_text: Which of the following ethical considerations is most important when a sport psychologist works with athletes on aggression management during their sessions?

options.text: Using confidential sessions, informed consent and athlete-centred goals
Publicly sharing all details of each session on social media
Ignoring the athlete's own goals and forcing only the coach's wishes
Applying extreme punishments for every minor emotional reaction

explanation: Sport psychologists work within ethical guidelines including confidentiality, informed consent and athlete-centred goal setting; these practices protect athlete wellbeing, support trust, and ensure that interventions address aggression constructively rather than through public shaming or coercion.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Public sharing violates confidentiality and ethics.
Ignoring athlete goals undermines trust and outcome.
Extreme punishments can escalate rather than reduce aggression.

question_text: Which of the following best describes the recommended long-term goal of aggression management programmes in youth sport based on sport psychology research and coaching practice?

options.text: Encourage as many hostile aggressive acts as possible in competition
Replace hostile aggression with assertive play and fair competition
Discourage all forms of physical contact permanently in sport
Remove all competitive elements from every youth sport event

explanation: The recommended long-term goal is to replace hostile aggression with assertive play and fair competition; athletes learn to channel energy within the rules, respect opponents and pursue sporting goals legitimately, which supports safety, enjoyment and healthy development in youth sport.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Encouraging hostile acts harms athletes and the sport.
Banning all contact removes the nature of many sports.
Removing competition entirely eliminates the sport itself.

question_text: Match each exercise adherence strategy in Column A with the specific barrier it most directly addresses listed in Column B and choose the option showing the right combination.

column_b.text: Lack of time in busy schedule

explanation: Short lunch-time workouts address lack of time; exercising with a partner addresses feelings of social isolation; tracking workouts with an app addresses difficulty seeing progress; varying activities across the week addresses boredom from repetitive routines over time.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Short workouts at lunch-time are a classic solution for time barriers.
A partner is the textbook remedy for social isolation.
Tracking progress is the typical remedy for invisible gains.

question_text: Match each cause of aggression in sport in Column A with the most appropriate description listed in Column B and choose the option showing the right combination.

column_b.text: External environmental provocation that raises arousal
Internal emotional state arising from thwarted aim
Perception that the rules are not applied fairly
Long-standing competitive tension between two sides

explanation: Frustration from blocked goals is an internal emotional state arising from thwarted aims; perceived unfair officiating reflects perception that the rules are not applied fairly; crowd hostility is an external environmental provocation that raises arousal; and rivalry is long-standing competitive tension between two sides.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Frustration from blocked goals is an internal emotional state.
Unfair officiating concerns perception of rule application.
Rivalry describes long-standing competitive tension.

question_text: Match each aggression control technique in Column A with the primary psychological mechanism through which it works as shown in Column B and choose the correct combination.

column_b.text: Reframes the meaning of provocative events
Changes the internal self-dialogue under pressure
Makes the cost of aggressive acts explicit

explanation: Progressive muscle relaxation reduces muscular and physiological tension; positive self-talk changes the internal self-dialogue under pressure; cognitive reappraisal reframes the meaning of provocative events; and clear disciplinary consequences make the cost of aggressive acts explicit for the athlete.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Progressive muscle relaxation primarily reduces body tension.
Self-talk works on internal dialogue during performance.
Cognitive reappraisal reframes the meaning of events.

question_text: Read the assertion and reason about exercise adherence among children and choose the option that best describes their logical relationship in school physical education practice.
assertion: Enjoyable and age-appropriate physical education classes in schools tend to improve long-term exercise adherence among children.
reason: Positive experiences in childhood build lifelong attitudes and habits toward physical activity and sport.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true; Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both are true but Reason does not correctly explain Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: Positive childhood experiences in physical education build lifelong positive attitudes and habits toward physical activity; children who enjoy activity are more likely to maintain it into adulthood, directly explaining the assertion's claim about long-term exercise adherence.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: The reason correctly links early experiences to lifelong habits.
Both statements are supported by health psychology research.
Enjoyable PE classes do have long-term adherence benefits.

question_text: Examine the assertion and reason about aggression in youth sport and choose the option that best represents their logical connection in coaching practice.
assertion: Coaches who model fair-play behaviour themselves tend to see lower levels of hostile aggression in their youth athletes over a season.
reason: Children and adolescents learn behaviour partly through observation and imitation of adults they look up to in sporting environments.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true; Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both are true but Reason does not correctly explain Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: Observational learning (social learning) suggests that children and adolescents learn behaviour partly by observing and imitating admired adults; when coaches model fair play, athletes are more likely to adopt similar values, directly explaining lower hostile aggression in their teams.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: The reason correctly invokes observational learning.
Both statements align with social learning research.
Modelling is a well-established influence on behaviour.

question_text: Consider the assertion and reason regarding exercise adherence and wellbeing in adults and choose the option that best describes their logical connection in health psychology.
assertion: Adults who maintain high exercise adherence over many years tend to enjoy better physical and mental wellbeing than inactive peers.
reason: Regular exercise produces cardiovascular, metabolic and neurochemical adaptations that reduce chronic disease risk and improve mood regulation.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true; Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both are true but Reason does not correctly explain Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: Cardiovascular, metabolic and neurochemical adaptations from sustained exercise reduce chronic disease risk and improve mood regulation; these cumulative changes over years directly translate into better physical and mental wellbeing, supporting the assertion about long-term adherent adults.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: The reason correctly lists the adaptive mechanisms.
Both statements align with extensive health research.
Long-term adherence drives cumulative wellbeing benefits.

question_text: Arrange the following steps of helping a previously sedentary adult improve exercise adherence in the correct chronological order recommended in sport and health psychology.

items.text: Assess current activity levels, preferences and health history
Design an individualised, enjoyable and realistic exercise plan
Build social support, tracking and reward structures
Review progress regularly and adjust the plan as needed

options.text: P → Q → R → S
Q → P → R → S
S → R → Q → P
R → P → S → Q

explanation: The adviser first assesses current activity levels, preferences and health history; designs an individualised, enjoyable and realistic exercise plan; builds social support, tracking and reward structures; and finally reviews progress regularly and adjusts the plan as needed over time.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Assessment must precede designing any exercise plan.
Plan design precedes building support and tracking.
Review and adjustment form the final closing step.

question_text: Arrange the following steps of an effective aggression control intervention for an adolescent athlete in the correct chronological order as applied by a sport psychologist over several weeks.

items.text: Build trust and understand typical aggression triggers
Teach relaxation, cognitive reappraisal and fair-play values
Rehearse skills in training with role-play and feedback
Apply skills in real matches and review outcomes with athlete

options.text: P → Q → R → S
Q → P → R → S
S → R → Q → P
R → P → S → Q

explanation: The psychologist first builds trust and understands typical aggression triggers; teaches relaxation, cognitive reappraisal and fair-play values; rehearses skills in training with role-play and feedback; and finally applies skills in real matches and reviews outcomes with the athlete.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Trust-building and trigger mapping precede skill teaching.
Skill teaching precedes supervised rehearsal in training.
Real-match application and review are the closing step.

question_text: State whether the following statement is true or false: Teaching fair-play values, relaxation skills and anger management to youth athletes can reduce hostile aggression during competitive play.

options.text: True
False

explanation: Structured teaching of fair-play values, relaxation skills and anger management gives youth athletes cognitive tools, emotional regulation strategies and clear behavioural norms; together these can reduce hostile aggression during competitive play and support sustained development.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: These tools give athletes healthy response options.
Both values and skills together target multiple causes.
Research supports their use in reducing hostile aggression.

question_text: Study the diagram summarising exercise adherence and aggression control strategies in sport; identify the strategy most directly aimed at reducing boredom and improving long-term adherence to a general exercise programme.

options.text: Introducing variety and cross-training into the routine
Teaching progressive muscle relaxation before matches
Applying clear disciplinary consequences for hostile aggression
Rehearsing cognitive reappraisal of competitive provocations

explanation: Introducing variety and cross-training into the routine directly addresses boredom and helps maintain long-term adherence to a general exercise programme; the other techniques listed are primarily designed to manage aggression or emotional arousal during competitive play in sport.
subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Progressive muscle relaxation targets emotional arousal, not boredom.
Disciplinary consequences target aggressive behaviour.
Cognitive reappraisal addresses competitive provocations.
